text: Aman has been described elsewhere as an Agagite or a Bugaean, neither of which terms can be explained with certainty. Macedonia would be regarded as a natural enemy of Persia at any time from 480b.c. onwards.
This verse, like the notice in 12.6 which connects Aman with the disloyal chamberlains, suggests the existence of a fuller version of the story, from which our narrative was abridged.
Literally, ‘are cancelled’, but this seems to have been technically impossible; cf. note on 8.8. ‘Left unheeded’ is the sense of the Septuagint Greek.
